summary: postinstall hook exfiltrates container/host reconnaissance to hardcoded Pipedream endpoint
details: hello244a@1.0.25 ships only a package.json whose postinstall script runs an inline Node one-liner that shells out via execSync to collect host/container reconnaissance (presence of /var/run/docker.sock, contents of /proc/1/mountinfo, listing of /dev, contents of /proc/1/cgroup) and POSTs the resulting JSON to https://eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net over https. The behavior fires automatically on npm install with no user interaction. The tarball contains no library or CLI code — the beacon is the entire package, so there is no legitimate purpose that could explain the data collection. This matches the shape of a sandbox/container-detection beacon used in dependency-confusion or targeted reconnaissance campaigns against internal build systems.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
